title: Compose contract test missing adversarial coverage for literal-bind / default-fallback bind on prometheus

scenarios.title: assertComposeContract rejects prometheus with literal 127.0.0.1: bind (spec 020 form)
scenarios.when: assertComposeContract is called with the fixture
scenarios.then: it returns a non-nil error mentioning `prometheus` AND at least one of [spec 049, spec 042, fail-loud, ${HOST_BIND_ADDRESS:?, ${HOST_BIND_ADDRESS:-127.0.0.1}, literal 127.0.0.1:]; the test docstring carries HL-RESCAN-010 attribution
scenarios.specSource: internal/deploy/compose_contract_test.go new TestComposeContract_AdversarialPrometheusLiteralBindAndFallbackForms function (pre-fix had no adversarial coverage for the literal 127.0.0.1: form on prometheus)
scenarios.redGreenProbe: go test -count=1 -v -run '^TestComposeContract_AdversarialPrometheusLiteralBindAndFallbackForms$' ./internal/deploy/... covers the literal-bind sub-case
scenarios.adversarial: YES — fails RED if the prometheus prefix check in assertComposeContract is relaxed to a too-loose substring that admits the literal-bind form

scenarios.title: assertComposeContract rejects prometheus with ${HOST_BIND_ADDRESS:-127.0.0.1}: default-fallback bind (forbidden by Gate G028)
scenarios.when: assertComposeContract is called with the fixture
scenarios.then: it returns a non-nil error mentioning `prometheus` AND at least one of [spec 049, spec 042, fail-loud, ${HOST_BIND_ADDRESS:?, ${HOST_BIND_ADDRESS:-127.0.0.1}, literal 127.0.0.1:]; the literal-bind sub-case is unaffected by softening the assertion to substring-contains (positive cross-check)
scenarios.specSource: internal/deploy/compose_contract_test.go new TestComposeContract_AdversarialPrometheusLiteralBindAndFallbackForms function (pre-fix had no adversarial coverage for the default-fallback ${HOST_BIND_ADDRESS:-127.0.0.1}: form on prometheus)
scenarios.redGreenProbe: go test -count=1 -v -run '^TestComposeContract_AdversarialPrometheusLiteralBindAndFallbackForms$' ./internal/deploy/... covers the default-fallback sub-case; relaxing strings.HasPrefix to strings.Contains(p, "${HOST_BIND_ADDRESS:") in the prometheus branch causes ONLY this sub-case to FAIL (literal-bind sub-case still PASSes — positive cross-check)
scenarios.adversarial: YES — fails RED if the prometheus prefix check in assertComposeContract is relaxed to a too-loose substring that admits the default-fallback form
